1.content:
Zack hated going to class, it was the worst part about going to college. He’d rather be doing literally anything else than sitting in a classroom for hours on end. And if going to class wasn’t enough, it had to be this dumb liberal arts prerequisite, “Experiential Humanities”.

Sitting through boring lectures was already mind-numbing, but having to listen to some loser SJWs talk about their feelings was even worse. Every class was a different speaker, each talking about their life experiences and the need for understanding and empathy and some other bullshit and the only thing Zack could think about was how much he wanted to get to his dorm room and turn on his Playstation.

Zack was a gamer and he said it with pride; He was sick and tired of the way people, mainly ugly dumb feminists, were trying to ruin his hobby. These days you couldn't to any game websites without some "woke" idiot complaining about games for being sexist or racist. It was so stupid. Of course, whenever he brought it up to anyone at this dumb school they rolled their eyes at him or shook their heads. They acted like HE was the bad guy. As if he wasn't the one surrounded by sheep.

Now, here he was, stuck in class, again, listening to some fat bitch drone on and on about body positivity or some shit. As if a body like hers was anything to feel positive about. He snickered to himself as he imagined manning the harpoons and taking down this land whale. He openly sneered as he took her in. She had to weigh at least 300 pounds, most of it blubber, quivering as she talked. Her fat arms were somehow bigger than her fat head. He would have told her to put some sleeves on, but he doubted there were any big enough to cover her. God she was annoying; Didn’t she know how gross and disgusting she was? Zack hated her from the moment she entered the room.

Stupid fat bitch. The way she talked like she was special. She wasn't special! It was too much for Zack to take.

“Why don’t you just lose weight?”, said Zack, interrupting the speaker in the middle of her talk.

He heard a few muted gasps and someone from behind him hissed, “Shut the fuck up!”

“Excuse me?”, said Kelly Mina, the guest speaker.

“Uh, yeah, if being fat is so hard and everything, why don’t you just lose weight?” Zack chided, with a tone that suggested this was the most obvious solution in the world.

“Do you really think it’s that simple?”, countered Kelly.

“Well yeah, if you don’t like something, then fix it.”

“Don’t you think I’m trying to do that? I don’t like the way society treats fat people, particularly fat woman, and I’m trying to open people’s minds to that.”

“Well wouldn’t it be easier to just change yourself?”

“Do you really think losing weight is that easy or that we should all just change ourselves to make other people more comfortable? For me personally, losing weight is difficult. If it was as easy as you’re suggesting, I would have done it when I was insecure. But once I learned to love myself, I realized that I didn’t need other people to validate my physical appearance.”

Her tone was calm, even and measured. It infuriated Zack.

“How hard is it to just eat less and exercise? Huh?”

That edge was creeping into his tone, the edge that made people not want to talk to him.

She laughed lightly. “OK, I think we’re done here. Thanks for the questions though.”

Other people joined in on the laughter. Zack could feel the heat rising in his face. He hated being talked down to and he hated it even more when it was a woman. And when it was a fat woman, it was practically the worst thing he could imagine.

“Yeah, OK, well have fun dying when you’re 30", he shot back, any attempt at not getting mad completely out the window.

“Oh, and you’re the model of good health?” She looked him up and down, smirking. “When’s the last time you took a shower? Or were you up all night 'gaming’?” She said it with such a tone of withering mockery that Zack lost what little self control he had left.

Everyone laughed again. Zack felt his face go red, again.

“Fat bitch!”, she screamed.

Everyone continued laughing, laughing at him. Kelly smiled, though her eyes looked sad.

“Tell me Zack, do you hate me because I’m a woman or because I’m fat, or is it both?”

“Fuck you!” he screamed, grabbing his bag and making his way to the door.

“I’d be careful about how you treat people Zack, you’d hate for it to come back on you.”

Zack slammed the door and hurried back to his dorm. He was so angry he didn’t know what to do with himself. It was of course, all her fault. That uppity fat bitch had made fun of him in front of everyone.
1.title: The Guest Lecturer

choices: A strange package addressed to him
Someone who hates Kelly Mina too
He doesn't get to see and gets knocked out
11.content:
Zack passed the time like he normally did when he was pissed off: playing an unreasonable amount of video games. This carried on into the night as he all but ignored his growling gut for food. The last thing he wanted to do was be near anyone after his utter embarrassment in the lecture. The dining hall was sure to be filled with the gossip of what had happened. College was fickle like that.

So, he remained in his dorm well past dinner time and into the dark of night.

It was around 11:42 PM when Zack finally pried himself from a rather involved game of Fortnight before shuffling over to his dorm's kitchen to find something he could it. It was well past the time his roommate was usually passed out for the school night, so he had the apartment mostly to himself as he scoured the fridge. Little did he know that there was something on its way that was about to change his life completely. A nightly encounter he'd least expect.

Just as Zack was about to settle for some leftover Toaster Strudels in the fridge, there came a knock at his door.

"Who the hell is that?" he muttered. Though he was used to staying up late, it was odd to have a guest over at this hour.

Zack approached the door and opened it to discover that what was interrupting his night of moping around was actually...
11.title: The Nightly Encounter

111.content:
"Hello there, Zack."

Zack's eyes trailed downwards at the sight of the last person he wanted to see. The fat bitch who had left him in such a foul mood to begin with.

"What the fuck do you want?" he snapped.

"Temper temper. And here I simply came over to have a polite conversation with you after our little spat in the lecture this afternoon. I've been looking for you all day. Have you really been doing nothing but playing video games in your room this whole time?"

"Fuck off!"

He then slammed the door on her and stomped off back to the kitchen. Still, she continued to knock and speak to him through the door.

"Zack, I'd much rather be civil about this. Can you please open this door and we can discuss this?"

"Leave me alone, fatty. Go to McDonalds or something. They gotta be open this late."

Zack heard a tired sigh followed by silence. There was a bright light from the other side of the door before the knob gently turned, revealing Kelly letting herself in and tucking something away in her pocketbook that Zack couldn't see.

"What the hell?" he asked. "I thought I locked that door."

"You did," Kelly replied as she adjusted her dress. "I opened it."

"How? You can't just break into someone's home. I'll call the cops! I wonder if they even make handcuffs large enough for people like you."

"Such anger," Kelly muttered. "I understand I embarrassed you this afternoon, but did I really deserve such hostility to begin with? That's what got me thinking and why I'm here in the first place. You seem to be a very unhappy person in general."

"Fuck you," he replied as he pulled out his cellphone. "I'm calling the police."

"Are you intimidated by me, Zack?"

"What? No."

"Then surely there's no need to get the police involved for little old me, is there?"

Zack scowled as he listened to the dial tone of his phone. The bitch had directly assaulted his pride as a man and if he called the cops then somehow he would look like the weak one here. Cursing again, he hung up the phone and stuffed it into his pocket.

"Fine. What do you want, bitch?"

"Well, you see it's more to do with what you want, Zack. I couldn't help but notice that you were very aggressive with me even though I don't think I said anything particularly offensive to you."

Zack scoffed. This lady was offensive enough simply by living.

"So, I asked a couple of your classmates and--"

"Wait a minute," he said. "Have you been spying on me? You creep."

"--And they all agreed," Kelly continued as if she hadn't heard him. "You're not well liked in your school, are you? Most people tend to avoid you and you're not sure why. So, you lash out at the easiest target. The people you deem the weakest. Those who are overweight and female, isn't that right?"

"Tsk. What are you, a psychiatrist?"

"No, but I am someone who can help you with your little problem. You see, I suffered from the same treatment from people for years. I blamed everyone else when in reality, I was the one having trouble accepting myself. I see the same conflict with you. And I want to help you."

"You want to help me?"

"Yes. You see, I'd like for you to..."
111.title: Kelly's at the Door

choices: "Take one of these pills every day."
Wear this amulet the whole time."
"Wear whatever clothes I tell you to."
1112.content:
"I'd like for you to try being my best friend," explained Kelly.

Zack waited a moment before busting out laughing.

"What? Best friend? Are you kidding me? Who would want to be best friends with you?"

Kelly frowned, though she didn't have quite the angry reaction Zack might have expected from her after such an outburst. It looked almost like she was sad, in a way.

"In exchange," she continued. "I am willing to give you anything your heart desires. Anything at all."

At that, Zack raised an eye. "Anything?"

"Anything. A new car. Your school loans paid off. The newest gaming console. Anything one thing, and just one thing, that you can think of will be yours if you go through with this."

"You're bullshitting me," Zack said, though he didn't hear it in her voice. "How can you afford all that? I thought you were just a motivational speaker."

"Oh, Zack. I am so much more than that, you have no idea. I have my ways. I can't promise it will be instantaneous like a genie's wish. But I will honor our deal if you go through with it."

"And how long do you want me to be your bud? Forever?"

"No. If it was forever then our relationship would basically be bought. This is more a lesson in how I can help you see things from a different perspective. To that end, I'd say we would need a month's time only."

"A month, huh? And what exactly does being your best friend entail?"

"No more staying up all night by yourself playing video games, for one. Outside of class, we'll spend most of your free time together doing whatever we feel like. Go out to eat. Maybe do some shopping. Do things that friends do together. To make sure you're fulfilling your quota though, I will require it that we do at least one activity per day of my choosing for the next thirty days. You have to go along with it without complaint. Afterwards, we'll decide what we can do together be it just hanging out at your dorm or whatever. Maybe you can show me how to play some of your games."

"As if," Zack huffed. "So I have to do whatever you want for thirty days and I get any wish I'd like?"

"That's correct."

"What if I say that I want you to strip naked and give a speech in front of my class about how much of a fat bitch you are?"

"Then if that is your wish," Kelly sighed. "Though something tells me by the end of it, you won't be nearly as keen on that idea."

"You're on."

"There is one more condition I have yet to mention," said Kelly as she reached into her pocketbook. "For the next thirty days you also have to..."
1112.title: The Best Friend

choices: Turn him into a fat girl by morning
Turn him into a fat girl slowly over the course of the month
Make him listen to Kelly's every word
Replace his soul with someone else's in the necklace
11122.content:
"You also have to wear this amulet the whole time," Kelly stated as she pulled out a golden necklace with a big ruby at its center. "And I will know when you aren't, believe me."

"Damn, that's some serious bling," Zack said as he accepted the jewelry and got a closer look. "Is this ruby real? It feels heavy enough to be."

"It most certainly is. And it's so much more than a simple ruby. But you'll discover that on your own, I'm sure. So, do we have a deal?"

With the amulet having exchanged hands, Kelly reached out and offered her own soft hand in the form of a shake. Zack huffed and accepted it with the necklace in his other hand.

"Fine, you got yourself a deal. I can still hide this thing if I wear it, right?"

"You can do whatever you wish with it. But I ask that you put it on now, please. And refrain from removing it."

Zack rolled his eyes and played with the golden chain a bit, undoing the clasp until he could reach behind his neck. It was a little challenging, having not been a jewelry guy before, but he was eventually able to work it out. The amulet dropped heavily down to his chest, there was a certain weight to it that almost felt like he was being dragged to the floor if he let it overcome him. There was a strange warmth from the ruby too, like it was made of fire. Though that had to be just a trick of the mind, right?

"Very good," Kelly said with a pleased smile across her lips. "Now then, it's late and I'd best be off. I'll come by in the morning to take you out for breakfast and see what develops."

"What do you mean by that?"

"Oh, nothing," Kelly bit at her knuckle like she was trying to keep herself from laughing. "Nothing at all. Good bye, Zack Smith."

Zack scowled as he slammed the door shut behind her. He had very good reason just to rip this stupid amulet off of himself. It's not like she would ever know, right? But then again, if she did find out somehow then that would be a waste of time with this whole deal. One month pretending to be the fat bitch's friend. That was easy enough, right? What did fat cows do anyway? Eat a bunch of ice cream? Go shopping at Torrid? This was going to be a breeze. And after a while, Zack didn't even notice the weight of the amulet anymore as he grabbed his pastries from the kitchen and slumped over to bed.

After messily devouring his strudels, Zack gave a big yawn and ripped off his jeans to serve as makeshift pajamas, throwing them on the floor with the rest of his dirty clothes. His room was a mess, much like the rest of his life, but he liked to keep it that way.

Unfortunately for Zack, his whole life was about to change entirely as the ruby on the amulet began to gently pulse while he slept. Little did he know that the amulet Kelly was having him wear would actually...
11122.title: The Amulet

info.brief_description: An obnoxious college student picked the wrong fat chick to insult.
info.description:
Zack is as unlikable as they come; He's rude, angry and quick to blow his top. One day he pisses off the wrong woman and Zach finds that he's in for some BIG changes. Will he be able to get his life back? Will he be stuck as fat girl forever?

Characters:

Zack Smith – Zack is 20 and doesn't have too many friends. He thinks it's because everyone else is the problem. The truth is that he's hard to be around and even harder to like. He has greasy brown hair, acne and stands about 5'10".

Kelly Mina – Kelly is a featured speaker in Zack's Humanities class. Kelly is body positive fat activist; Bullied for her weight for most of her life, she managed to overcome her own feelings of insecurity and tries to help other people learn to love themselves.

Other characters will be added as they're discovered.

**This is a M2BBW story. Sex is allowed. Scat/Vore is not. Chapters should be proofread before being added. Chapters should be at least one paragraph. Poor grammar will be deleted.**
